key_insight: The paper shows that thick single-crystal diamond membranes can be deeply etched and then room-temperature bonded onto silica without losing optical functionality, enabling scalable diamond-on-silica heterostructures.
engineering_relevance: This provides a contamination-free fabrication route for quantum photonics and sensing devices that need high-aspect-ratio diamond features on an insulating substrate.
method: Single-layer SiO2 hard mask with multi-step oxygen ICP-RIE for deep diamond etching
Achieved 16 µm through-etch of NV-doped diamond with 15:1 diamond-to-SiO2 selectivity
Used O2 plasma activation plus sodium silicate interlayer for room-temperature bonding to SiO2
evidence_signal: The abstract reports complete through-etching of a 16 µm-thick membrane, well-defined sidewalls, conserved roughness, negligible micromasking, and no detectable parasitic photoluminescence after bonding.
watch_item: The process depends on precise control of deep ICP-RIE and surface activation/bonding conditions, which may affect yield and uniformity when scaled further.
paper_title: Scalable Fabrication of Diamond-on-Silica Heterostructures via High-Selectivity Deep ICP-RIE and Room-Temperature Bonding
